titleParsingSites.reason: Last-resort fallback parses the position title when location/address/region fields are empty.
titleParsingSites.suggestedReplacement: Keep as diagnostics-only: the result is already tagged locationSource='job_name' — downstream gates must treat that tag as low-confidence instead of equal to Position.Location.

titleParsingSites.reason: P214 derived posting geography from the stored position title and never resolved the applied Breezy Position object.
titleParsingSites.suggestedReplacement: Resolve candidate.positionId via fetchBreezyPositionById and use Position.Location (job.city/job.state, locationSource != 'job_name'); fall back to title parsing only as explicit low-confidence evidence.

titleParsingSites.function: main (parseLocationFromJobName fallback for applied job location)
titleParsingSites.reason: Coverage audit parses the position name when the Breezy jobs catalog lacks the position (e.g. closed positions missing from the published list).
titleParsingSites.suggestedReplacement: Fetch the specific position by id (fetchBreezyPositionById works for closed positions) before falling back to the title.

titleParsingSites.reason: Posting-quality scoring judges geography from the title.
titleParsingSites.suggestedReplacement: Use the position's normalized city/state + locationSource from the jobs snapshot; score 'title parseable' separately from 'has Position.Location'.

titleParsingSites.reason: Reconciliation compares title-derived location with stored fields.
titleParsingSites.suggestedReplacement: Compare against Position.Location fields; keep title parse only to flag title/location drift.

titleParsingSites.reason: Publish review derives expected location from the draft title.
titleParsingSites.suggestedReplacement: Validate the draft's location block directly and require city+state before publish, instead of accepting a parseable title.
